stimulus: There are nearly thirty national parks in Costa Rica. A student in a geography class is studying Tortuguero National Park on the northeastern coast. The student wants to compare the total area covered by Tortuguero with the total areas of various other national parks in the country. Looking at the table, the student finds that the total area of Tortuguero is 119.4 square miles, while the total area of Rincón de la Vieja is ______
stem: Which choice most effectively uses data from the table to complete the comparison?
rationale:
Choice A is the best answer. The table shows that Rincón de la Vieja has a total area of 54.7 square miles.

Choice B is incorrect; 164.8 square miles is the area of Corcovado. Choice C is incorrect; 26.1 square miles is the area of Manuel Antonio. Choice D is incorrect; 192.5 square miles is the area of Chirripó.
